summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Failover Engine PHASE 4.4. All 15 API endpoints tested successfully with 36/36 tests passing. The module includes infrastructure risk protection with exchange health monitoring, latency tracking, rate limit monitoring, connection guard, and system status transitions (NORMAL, DEGRADED, FAILOVER, EMERGENCY). All failover mechanisms working correctly.
passed_tests: GET /api/failover/health - returns status healthy, version phase_4.4, and 5 components
GET /api/failover/status - system status with exchange health data and execution flags
GET /api/failover/exchange/{exchange} - BINANCE/BYBIT/OKX specific status (case insensitive)
GET /api/failover/latency - latency statistics for all exchanges or specific exchange
GET /api/failover/rate-limit - rate limit status and monitoring with utilization percentages
GET /api/failover/connection - connection status monitoring for REST/WebSocket connections
GET /api/failover/events - recent events with optional limit and severity filtering
GET /api/failover/history - event history from DB with filtering options
GET /api/failover/stats - failover statistics with custom period support
GET /api/failover/exchange-comparison - historical and current exchange comparison
GET /api/failover/config - configuration with latency/error/rate limit thresholds
POST /api/failover/record - record single request with latency analysis and grading
POST /api/failover/batch-record - batch record up to 100 requests with results
POST /api/failover/emergency - trigger emergency mode with appropriate actions
POST /api/failover/reset - reset system to NORMAL mode
System status transitions: NORMAL -> EMERGENCY -> NORMAL flow working correctly
Data validation: default values, severity filters, event type filters working
Integration workflow: record requests -> latency stats -> monitoring cycle complete
Emergency mode triggers: FREEZE_EXECUTION, CANCEL_PENDING_ORDERS, SECURE_POSITIONS
Throttle and position size factors calculated correctly based on system status
Exchange health scoring includes status, health_score, latency_ms, error_rate
Rate limit monitoring with warning (70%) and critical (90%) thresholds
Connection health monitoring with heartbeat tracking and reconnection attempts
Event tracking with proper severity levels (INFO, WARNING, CRITICAL)
Latency grading: EXCELLENT < 50ms, GOOD < 100ms, NORMAL < 200ms, DEGRADED < 500ms, POOR < 800ms, CRITICAL > 800ms
